message.content: Rogue Domain Controller (T1207) involves adversaries registering a rogue Domain Controller (DC) to manipulate Active Directory (AD) data. Using tools like DCShadow, attackers can simulate DC behavior, injecting and replicating changes into the AD infrastructure, including credentials and keys. This requires Administrator privileges or the KRBTGT hash. The technique can bypass system logging and security monitors, complicating detection and forensic analysis. Adversaries may use it to alter replication metadata, perform SID-History Injection, and manipulate AD objects to establish backdoors for persistence. Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unauthorized DC registrations, ensuring robust logging and alerting mechanisms, and regularly auditing AD configurations and permissions.
